mappings.rationaleAI: Directly addresses the evolution of lungs as respiratory organs in vertebrates, the mechanics of ventilation, the vagus nerve's role in respiratory control, and respiratory adaptations across environments including the diaphragm in mammals and unidirectional airflow in birds.

Covers blood components (red cells, white cells, platelets, plasma), blood typing and transfusion, Harvey's discovery of circulation, and the history of understanding cardiovascular physiology -- directly relevant to circulatory fluid media and vertebrate circulatory systems.
Cephalopods possess three hearts, blue copper-based blood, and jet propulsion -- illustrating invertebrate circulatory systems with distinctive formed elements and transport mechanisms.

The fish-tetrapod transition covers the evolution of air-breathing and adaptation to terrestrial environments, directly relevant to the development of vertebrate respiratory organs (gills to lungs) and adaptation to special environmental conditions.

The nervous system episode covers reflex arcs, the autonomic nervous system (sympathetic and parasympathetic), and neural control pathways -- directly relevant to the neural reflexes controlling respiration.
Photosynthesis covers the fundamental process of oxygen production and carbon dioxide consumption by plants, the complementary side of the gas exchange equation described in A.1.
